title: Gilead Less M&A Happy Now but Door Still Open for ‘Compelling’ Opportunities
snippet: Gilead Sciences has inked three deals this year so far totaling $14.77 billion, a marked escalation of the company’s usual M&A pace. Executives detailed the rationale for buying Arcellx, Ouro Medicine and Tubulis GmbH and whether they are interested in further deals.

title: 10 Companies Hiring in Texas Now
snippet: Looking for a biopharma job in Texas? Check out the BioSpace list of 10 companies hiring life sciences professionals like you.

title: Novavax Shareholder Vows To Fight CEO Pay Package, End ‘Dogmatic Leadership’
snippet: "Your tenure has overseen a DESTRUCTION of shareholder value—a direct result of chronic operational shortcomings and failures on fiduciary duty," Shah Capital wrote to Novavax’s leadership in an open letter published Wednesday.

title: Insmed Scraps Skin Plans for Lung Disease Drug as Competitors Make Headway
snippet: After a Phase 2 flop, Brinsupri exits the race to market for the chronic skin disease hidradenitis suppurativa, but other companies, including Incyte, Novartis and UCB, have recently notched clinical and regulatory victories.

title: Merck Chopped $1B Off Terns’ Offer After Seeing Updated Leukemia Data
snippet: Another bidder, which remains unidentified, dropped out of the bidding process. Analysts at William Blair now think it unlikely that another suitor could offer a counter-proposal to Merck’s outstanding $6.7 billion acquisition offer.

title: Lilly’s Foundayo Nod Launches New Battle With Novo, M&A Mania Continues, Tariffs Hit Pharma
snippet: The approval of Eli Lilly’s oral obesity drug officially ignites an intense competition with Novo Nordisk’s oral Wegovy; Gilead Sciences and Neurocrine Biosciences keep the M&A train chugging; Trump hits pharma with his long promised tariffs, and the FDA proposes many changes with 2027 budget.

title: When a Good Boss Is Bad for Your Career
snippet: Some bosses stretch you. Others make work more bearable. Both can earn your loyalty. Only one is building your future. Leadership coach Angela Justice explains how to tell the difference.

title: As CGT Manufacturing Scales Up, Automation and Collaboration Become Essential
snippet: As cell and gene therapy developers face rising pressure to produce therapies faster and at lower cost, the industry is leaning on robotics, digital systems and partnerships to bridge the gap between innovation and delivery.

title: Retrospective, single-center analysis of second allogeneic stem cell transplantation versus non-transplant regimens for acute myeloid leukemia patients with relapse after first transplantation.
snippet: Relapse of acute myeloid leukemia (AML) after first allogeneic stem cell transplantation (ASCT) is associated with a poor prognosis. While previous studies have focused solely on outcomes after a second ASCT (ASCT2) in this patient population, we aimed to compare the therapeutic efficacy of ASCT2 with transplant-free regimens. We retrospectively compiled a....

title: An Artificial Intelligence Optimized Hepatic Differentiation Unveils NR5A2 and AP-1 Transcriptional Regulation in Hepatic Maturation.
snippet: The generation of hepatocyte-like cells (HLCs) from human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs) holds great promise for drug discovery and cell-based therapy for liver disease. However, current differentiation protocols are complicated and unstable, and the underlying gene regulatory mechanisms of hepatic differentiation remain incompletely defined. Here, we devel...

title: Epigenetic Modulation of the Gut-Muscle Axis in Pompe Disease: Microbiota Fingerprints to Cellular and Molecular Pathomechanisms.
snippet: Inter-organ cross-talk is increasingly recognised as a fundamental determinant in the pathogenesis of neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders, modulating neuroinflammation, protein misfolding, and cellular dysfunction through systemic mediators such as cytokines, adipokines, and growth factors.
